questions.question:
What is the time complexity for the following C module? Assume that \(n \gt 0\).


int module(int n)
{
    if (n == 1)
        return 1;
    else
        return (n + module(n-1));
}


(ISRO CSE 2014)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Which of the following is true with respect to Reference?

(ISRO CSE 2014)
options:
B.

A reference needs an explicit dereferencing mechanism

C.

A reference can be reassigned after it is established
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the C function given below

 int f(int j)
{
static int i = 50;
int k;
if (i == j)
{
printf("something");
k = f(i);
return 0;
}
else return 0;
}
Which one of the following is TRUE?

(GATE CSE 2014 SET-2)
options:
A.

The function returns 0 for all values of j.

B.

The function prints the string something for all values of j.

D.

The function will exhaust the runtime stack or run into an infinite loop when j = 50
questions.correct_answer:
D.

The function will exhaust the runtime stack or run into an infinite loop when j = 50
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the following function

 double f (double x) {
if ( abs (x*x - 3) < 0. 01) return x;
else return f (x / 2 + 1.5/x);
}

questions.question:
Consider the function func shown below:

 int func(int num) {
int count = 0;
while (num) {
    count++;
    num >> = 1;
}
return (count);
}
The value returned by func(435)is __________.

(GATE CSE 2014 SET-2)

questions.question:
Consider the following psuedocode:

x: integer := 1  
y: integer := 2   
procedure add  
    x:= x + y    
procedure second (P: Procedure)  
    x: integer := 2      
    p()   
procedure first  
    y: integer := 3
    second (add) 
first () 
write_integer(x)
What does it print if the language uses dynamic scoping with deep binding?

(ISRO CSE 2013)

questions.question:
What is the return value of f(p,p), if the value of p is initialized to 5 before the call? Note that the first parameter is passed by reference, whereas the second parameter is passed by value.

 int f (int &x, int c) {
  c = c - 1;
  if (c==0) return 1;
   x = x + 1;
return f(x,c) * x;
}


(GATE CSE 2013)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the following C code segment.


int a, b, c = 0;
void prtFun(void);
main( )
{ static int a = 1; /* Line 1 */
   prtFun( );
   a += 1;
   prtFun( );
   printf(" \n %d %d ", a, b);
}
void prtFun(void)
{ static int a = 2; /* Line 2 */
   int b = 1;
   a += ++b;
   printf(" \n %d %d ", a, b);
}
What output will be generated by the given code segment if:

Line 1 is replaced by auto int a = 1;
Line 2 is replaced by register int a = 2;

(GATE CSE 2012)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the following recursive C function that takes two arguments

 unsigned int foo(unsigned int n, unsigned int r) {
  if (n  > 0) return (n%r +  foo (n/r, r ));
  else return 0;
}
What is the return value of the function foo when it is called as foo (513, 2)?

(GATE CSE 2011)

questions.question:
What is the value printed by the following C program?

 #include < stdio.h >
int f(int * a, int n)
{
    if(n<=0)return 0;
    else if(*a% 2 ==0) return *a+f(a+1,n-1);
    else return *a-f(a+1,n-1);
}
int main ( )
{
    int a[ ] {12, 7, 13, 4, 11, 6};
    printf ("%d", f(a,6));
    return 0;
}


(GATE CSE 2010)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

 #include < stdio.h >
void f (int *p, int * q) {
       p=q;
       *p=2;
}

int main ( ){
     f(&i, & j);
     printf( "%d%d \ n", i,j);
     return 0;
}

questions.question:
Consider the following code written in a pass-by-reference language like FORTRAN and these statements about the code. 

 subroutine swap(ix,iy) 
     it = ix 
L1 : ix = iy 
L2 : iy = it 
    end 
    ia = 3 
    ib = 8 
    call swap (ia, ib+5)
    print *, ia, ib 
    end

S1: The compiler will generate code to allocate a temporary nameless cell, initialize it to 13, and pass the address of the cell to swap
S2: On execution the code will generate a runtime error on line L1
S3: On execution the code will generate a runtime error on line L2
S4: The program will print 13 and 8
S5: The program will print 13 and -2
Exactly the following set of statement(s) is correct:

(ISRO CSE 2009)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the program below:

 # include < stdio.h >
int fun(int n, int * f_p) {
   int t, f;
   if (n <=1) {
         *f_p =1;
          return 1;
   }
   t = fun (n-1, f_p);
   f = t+*f_p;
   *f_p = t;
   return f;
}
int main() {
    int x = 15;
    printf ("%d\ n", fun(5,& x));
    return 0;
}
The value printed is

(GATE CSE 2009)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow
